# Project Charta
## Context and Scope
This project is situated in the domain of **retail analytics and data-driven decision-making**. The focus lies on analysing customer purchasing behaviour in grocery retail using the **Instacart Online Grocery Shopping Dataset (2017)**.

The project addresses the topic of **market basket analysis**, with the goal of identifying patterns in customer purchases, particularly which products are frequently bought together.

The key questions to be answered are:

- Which products are most frequently purchased?
- Which products are commonly bought together?
- What purchasing patterns exist across product categories?
- How can these insights be used to optimise **product placement in a physical grocery store**?

The expected benefit of the project is to support **data-driven store layout decisions**, leading to:

- Increased cross-selling
- Improved customer experience
- Higher sales potential

The visualization product will be published as a **web-based documentation and visualization (Quarto website)**.

---

## Project Objectives and Success Criteria

### Objectives (Stakeholder Perspective)

- Enable stakeholders to **understand purchasing patterns** and product relationships  
- Support **data-driven decisions for store layout optimisation**  
- Identify **high-impact product combinations** for strategic placement  
- Provide an **intuitive and accessible visualisation** of complex purchasing data  

---

### Success Criteria

**Qualitative:**
- Users can easily interpret purchasing patterns without needing technical knowledge  
- Visualisations clearly highlight relationships between products  
- Insights can be translated into actionable store layout decisions  

**Quantitative:**
- At least **3 clear and actionable insights** derived from the data  
- Visualisations understandable within **< 30 seconds** (first impression clarity)  
- At least **2 different types of product relationships** identified (e.g. pairs, categories)  

---

### Out of Scope

- Real-time recommendation systems  
- Implementation of actual store layout changes  
- Machine learning prediction models  
- Customer-level personalization  

---

## Stakeholder Analysis

| Stakeholder       | Role             | Goals                                   |
|-------------------|------------------|-----------------------------------------|
| Retail Manager    | Decision-maker   | Optimise store layout and increase sales |
| Category Manager  | Product strategy | Understand product relationships         |
| Data Analyst      | Technical user   | Explore and validate patterns            |
| Customers (indirect) | End users    | Improved shopping experience             |

---

## User Analysis

### Persona 1: Retail Manager (Non-technical)

- **Data literacy:** Low–Medium  
- **Goal:** Improve store layout and increase revenue  
- **Device:** Laptop / Tablet  
- **Usage frequency:** Occasional  

**User Tasks:**
- Understand which products to place together  
- Make layout decisions  
- Present findings to stakeholders  

**Pains:**
- Complex data is hard to interpret  
- No clear insights from raw data  
- Time constraints  

**Gains:**
- Clear recommendations  
- Easy-to-understand visuals  
- Fast decision support  

---

### Persona 2: Data Analyst (Technical)

- **Data literacy:** High  
- **Goal:** Explore and validate purchasing patterns  
- **Device:** Laptop  
- **Usage frequency:** Frequent  

**User Tasks:**
- Analyse product relationships  
- Validate hypotheses  
- Explore data interactively  

**Pains:**
- Large dataset complexity  
- Lack of structured visual exploration tools  

**Gains:**
- Interactive exploration  
- Clear pattern detection  
- Efficient analysis workflow  

---

## Situation Assessment

### Resources
- Dataset: Instacart (Kaggle)  
- Tools: Python, Pandas, Matplotlib/Seaborn, Quarto  
- Team: Student group  
- Infrastructure: Local development + GitHub  

### Constraints
- Limited project time  
- No real store validation  
- Dataset is static and anonymised  

### Risks
- Dataset too large → performance issues  
- Insights may be too complex to communicate  
- Overly technical visualisations  

---

## Visualization Concept

### Product Form
- Interactive **data story / dashboard (Quarto website)**  

---

### Visual Encodings
- Bar charts → most purchased products  
- Heatmaps → co-purchased products  
- Network graphs → product relationships  
- Category charts → distribution across departments  

---

### Interactivity
- Filtering by product category  
- Highlighting relationships  
- Hover tooltips  

---

### Narrative and Annotation
- Clear storytelling structure:
  1. Overview of purchasing behaviour  
  2. Deep dive into product relationships  
  3. Recommendations for store layout  

- Use of annotations to highlight key insights  

---

### Target Medium
- Web-based (Quarto documentation site)  

---

### Value Justification

**Cognitive and analytical value:**
- Helps detect patterns and relationships between products  
- Simplifies complex datasets  

**Communicative value:**
- Enables non-technical stakeholders to understand insights  
- Supports decision-making  

**Experiential and aesthetic value:**
- Clean, engaging design  
- Interactive exploration increases engagement  

---


## Project Plan
Divide the project into individual phases, describe them briefly and draw up a preliminary timetable, e.g. as a Gantt chart:

```{mermaid}
%%| label: fig-project-plan
%%| fig-cap: Preliminary project plan in the form of a Gantt chart.
gantt
    title Optimising Product Placement Using Grocery Purchase Data
    dateFormat YYYY-MM-DD
    tickInterval 7day

    section Project Understanding
        Research & Context        :a1, 2026-03-20, 3d
        Stakeholders & Users      :a2, 2026-03-23, 4d
        Situation Analysis        :a3, 2026-03-27, 2d
        Charta Finalised          : milestone, m1, 2026-03-29, 1d

    section Data Exploration
        Data Cleaning             :a4, 2026-03-30, 5d
        EDA                       :a5, 2026-04-04, 7d
        Key Insights              :a6, 2026-04-11, 3d
        Data Report               : milestone, m2, 2026-04-14, 1d

    section Visual Design
        Concept Design            :a7, 2026-04-15, 4d
        Prototyping               :a8, 2026-04-19, 7d
        Dashboard / Data Story    :a9, 2026-04-26, 10d
        Review & Iteration        :a10, 2026-05-06, 4d

    section Evaluation
        Testing                   :a11, 2026-05-10, 5d
        Improvements              :a12, 2026-05-15, 5d

    section Deployment
        Documentation             :a13, 2026-05-20, 5d
        Publishing                :a14, 2026-05-25, 2d

    section Presentation
        Slides                    :a15, 2026-05-27, 3d
        Rehearsal                 :a16, 2026-05-30, 2d
        Final Presentation        : milestone, m3, 2026-06-01, 1d
```

See [Mermaid syntax for Gantt charts](https://mermaid.js.org/syntax/gantt.html). It might not be displayed correctly in Safari &#8594; use Chrome. [Live editor with export functionality](https://mermaid.live/)

## Roles and Contact Details
## Roles and Contact Details

| Name                     | Role           | Responsibilities                                        | Contact                            |
|--------------------------|---------------|----------------------------------------------------------|------------------------------------|
| Lara Pestrin             | Data Analyst  | Data acquisition, cleaning and exploratory data analysis | peslar01@students.zhaw.ch          |
| Jonathan Kull            | Data Analyst  | Data acquisition, cleaning and exploratory data analysis | kulljon1@students.zhaw.ch          |
| Veprim Mehmeti           | Data Analyst  | Data acquisition, cleaning and exploratory data analysis | mehmevep@students.zhaw.ch          |
| Course Lecturers         | Supervisors   | Provide methodological guidance, feedback and evaluation of the project | via course platform |
